info.title: Secure Incident API
info.description: Core API draft for the first implementation stage of Secure Incydent. This version follows the domain rule that a user can belong to many projects, but only within one organization or in private projects.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Project creation, listing, membership, and details.

tags.name: Categories
tags.description: Project-scoped categories used to classify incidents.

tags.name: Incidents
tags.description: Incident reporting, workflow, comments, and visible logs.

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: User profile and optional presentation data.

tags.name: Invitations
tags.description: Extended invite handling for organizations and projects.

post.description: Creates a new user account. After registration the user may create one organization, join one existing organization, or continue with private projects only.

post.summary: Log in user and create a session
post.description: Authenticates the user and establishes a session, for example by setting an HTTP-only cookie.
Initiates password reset flow. The response should not reveal whether the account exists.
Finalizes password reset using a previously issued reset token/code.

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated user resolved from the session, including the current organization id if the user belongs to one.

post.description: Creates an organization and assigns the current user as owner/admin. This should fail if the user already belongs to another organization.

409.description: User already belongs to an organization

get.description: Returns the single organization the current user belongs to. If the user does not belong to any organization, return null or 404 depending on backend convention.

404.description: User does not belong to any organization

post.description: Generates a one-time or limited-use invite for joining the current organization.
Uses a valid organization invite token to join the user's single allowed organization. Should fail if the user already belongs to another organization.

409.description: User already belongs to an organization

post.description: Creates a project. Project scope can be PRIVATE or ORGANIZATION. A user may have many projects, but organization projects must belong to the user's single organization.

get.summary: List projects visible to current user
get.description: Returns private projects and organization projects visible to the current user.
Returns project metadata visible to the current user.

patch.description: Allows authorized users to update project name and description.

get.description: Returns project members with their single assigned role in that project.

post.description: Adds a user to a project and assigns exactly one role in that project.

patch.description: Changes the single assigned role of a project member.

get.description: Returns categories available in a project.

post.description: Creates a category available for incidents in the project.

get.description: Returns roles available in the given project.

post.description: Creates a custom role with boolean permissions in the given project.

get.description: Returns incidents visible to the current user in the selected project. Supports filtering and pagination.

post.description: Creates a new incident in the selected project. The field name is title, not name.

get.summary: List incidents reported by current user
get.description: Convenience endpoint for the 'my reported incidents' view.

get.summary: List incidents assigned to current user
get.description: Convenience endpoint for the 'my assigned incidents' view.
Returns main incident details without comments.

patch.description: Updates the incident status and should create a visible entry in incident logs.
Updates the primary assigned solver of the incident.
Updates the main category assigned to the incident.

get.description: Returns comments visible in incident details view.

post.description: Adds a new comment to the incident. Author identity must come from session.

get.description: Returns logs visible to the reporting user and project participants. These logs include comments, status changes, and assignee changes only.
Returns editable profile data of the current user.

patch.description: Updates optional user profile fields such as bio or display preferences.

get.summary: Search users by username or partial text
get.description: Convenience endpoint useful for assignment dialogs and member lookup.

post.description: Generates an invite token that lets a user join a specific project directly.
Accepts a project invite and joins the current user to the project.

get.description: Lets frontend validate an invite link and preview whether it grants access to an organization or a project.

delete.description: Deletes a category when it is not used or after a safe migration.

get.description: Returns details of one role with all permissions.

patch.description: Updates role name or boolean permissions.

post.description: Convenience endpoint if frontend prefers a dedicated close action instead of generic status patch.
Allows the current solver to request reassignment without directly removing themselves.
Optional extension if you later want to formally model helper participants.

delete.description: Removes a previously added helper from the incident.

get.description: Extended reporting endpoint for user history across projects in the user's single organization and/or private projects.
